title: uva-ursi × strigosa
intro: Uva-ursi × strigosa, also known as bearberry, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross-breeding of Arctostaphylos uva-ursi and Arctostaphylos strigosa, both belonging to the heath family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of arbutin. The health benefits of bearberry include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Bearberry can be used in herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for various health issues.
